title: Add corkboard-style scene cards for story planning
description:
## Problem
Linear document editing alone makes it hard to visualize narrative flow and quickly spot pacing or sequencing issues.

## Target user
Plot-driven writers who plan scenes before or during drafting.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase usage of planning views before drafting sessions.
- Improve completion rate of planned scenes to drafted scenes.

## Scope
- Introduce a corkboard/planning view with one card per scene.
- Card fields: scene title, chapter, short summary, status (planned/drafting/done), and drag reorder position.
- Clicking a card opens that scene in the editor.
- Keep card order synced with chapter/scene structure order.

## Non-goals
- Full kanban workflow customization.
- Reader/community feedback features.
- Mobile-native app redesign.

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can switch between editor view and corkboard view.
- Scene cards can be reordered and updates are persisted.
- Scene status changes are saved and visible in both corkboard and structure sidebar.
- Opening a card routes to the correct scene draft.

## Priority rationale
A corkboard is a high-value differentiator for structured fiction writing and aligns with the primary inspiration product behavior.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Build persistent Story Library with create, rename, archive, and resume
description:
## Problem
The app currently shows empty placeholders and does not let writers manage real stories. Without persistent story records and quick resume, users cannot build a daily writing habit or trust the app for ongoing projects.

## Target user
Solo fiction writers drafting one or more long-form stories who need a reliable home screen and library to manage works-in-progress.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase weekly active writers who return to an existing story
- At least 70% of writing sessions start from Quick Resume or Stories list after launch
- Reduce first-session drop-off between story creation and first edit

## Scope
- Add story persistence layer for metadata (title, updatedAt, archive flag)
- Stories page supports create, rename, archive/unarchive, and delete-confirmation guardrail
- Home dashboard shows Recent Stories and Quick Resume based on last edited items
- Clicking any story opens `/story/[id]` for editing
- Empty states include clear primary actions (Create story / Restore archived)

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-user collaboration
- Publishing/sharing workflows
- AI writing assistance

## Acceptance criteria
- User can create a new story and see it appear in Stories and Home Recent list immediately
- Renaming a story persists across reload and navigation
- Archived stories are hidden from default list and recoverable from an archive filter
- Quick Resume always points to most recently edited non-archived story
- Deleting requires explicit confirmation and removes the story from all lists

## Priority rationale
This is the minimum product surface required to make the app usable beyond a demo. It unlocks all downstream drafting and structure features by making stories first-class persisted objects.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Add chapter and scene navigator with drag-and-drop ordering
description:
## Problem
Writers need structure while drafting, but the current editor is a single undifferentiated text area. Without chapters/scenes, users cannot plan pacing, reorder narrative beats, or navigate long manuscripts efficiently.

## Target user
Novelists and serial fiction writers managing multi-chapter drafts who need scene-level organization similar to Living Writer clarity, without extra clutter.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase average number of scenes created per active manuscript
- Improve navigation efficiency (time to jump to target scene)
- Reduce manuscript abandonment after chapter 1

## Scope
- Add left-side manuscript tree: chapters with nested scenes
- Support create/edit/delete for chapters and scenes
- Drag-and-drop reorder for chapters and scenes (with keyboard-accessible fallback controls)
- Editor writes to selected scene, not global document blob
- Persist ordering and selection across reloads

## Non-goals
- Timeline visualization
- Character/worldbuilding ontology
- Collaboration features

## Acceptance criteria
- User can create chapter + scene and switch between scenes without data loss
- Reordering scenes updates persisted order and survives refresh
- Scene title updates reflect in navigator and editor header
- Deleting a scene requires confirmation and cannot silently remove content
- Keyboard users can reorder without mouse-only interactions

## Priority rationale
Structured drafting is a core differentiator versus plain document tools and is foundational for future outlining, continuity, and revision intelligence. Shipping this now moves the app from generic editor to story-first product.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship reliable autosave + crash recovery with visible save state
description:
## Problem
Writers lose trust immediately when they fear losing words. The current editing experience has no visible save confidence, no recovery workflow, and no resilience guidance when network/storage failures occur.

## Target user
Daily writers with long sessions who need confidence that every sentence is safe, especially on laptops/mobile where interruptions are common.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce reported lost-work incidents to near zero
- Increase average session length and return rate
- Improve user trust score for reliability after first week of use

## Scope
- Autosave current scene content on idle interval and on navigation/unload
- Display explicit save state in editor header (`Saving…`, `Saved`, `Save failed`)
- Keep local recovery snapshot for unsynced edits and offer one-click restore
- Add non-blocking error banner with retry action when save fails
- Record last-saved timestamp for user reassurance

## Non-goals
- Full version history UI
- Multi-device merge conflict resolution
- Real-time collaboration editing

## Acceptance criteria
- Typing triggers autosave without manual action
- Save state updates correctly during save lifecycle
- On simulated crash/reload, user can restore unsaved local changes
- Failed save shows actionable retry path and does not silently discard edits
- Last-saved timestamp is visible and updates after successful save

## Priority rationale
Reliability is table stakes for writer retention and prerequisite for premium features. Shipping visible trust signals and recovery now reduces churn risk before expanding advanced tooling.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Add worldbuilding panel with linked story elements
description:
## Problem
Writers need fast access to character/location/lore context while drafting, but context switching to separate notes breaks flow.

## Target user
Writers building rich fictional worlds with recurring entities and rules.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase count of stories using reusable worldbuilding entities.
- Reduce context-switching events away from active drafting views.

## Scope
- Add worldbuilding entities: character, location, and lore item (minimal schema).
- Add a side panel in editor to browse/search entities and pin relevant items per scene.
- Enable linking entities to scenes and viewing linked entities inline in scene context.
- Keep panel collapsible to preserve distraction-free writing space.

## Non-goals
- Advanced map builders, language generators, or magic-system simulation.
- Public wiki publishing for story worlds.
- Real-time collaborative world editing.

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create/edit/delete character, location, and lore entities.
- Writer can link/unlink entities to a scene from the editor.
- Scene view shows linked entities without leaving the draft context.
- Panel can be collapsed and reopened without losing draft state.

## Priority rationale
This captures a key Campfire-inspired advantage while keeping the writing surface clean, supporting differentiation beyond a plain document editor.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Create lightweight timeline view for chronological consistency
description:
## Problem
Writers struggle to track chronology across scenes, causing continuity errors and rework late in the drafting process.

## Target user
Novelists managing multi-thread stories with time-sensitive events.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce reported continuity-conflict edits during revision.
- Increase number of stories using timeline metadata per scene.

## Scope
- Add optional timeline metadata to scenes (date/time or relative order).
- Provide a timeline view listing scenes in chronological order independent of manuscript order.
- Flag obvious chronology conflicts (e.g., duplicate impossible ordering within same storyline tag).
- Enable click-through from timeline entry to scene editor.

## Non-goals
- Full calendar app integration.
- Complex simulation of travel/logistics constraints.
- AI auto-fixing timeline conflicts.

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can set and edit timeline metadata for a scene.
- Timeline view renders scenes by chronological order and supports navigation to scene drafts.
- At least one deterministic chronology conflict rule is enforced and surfaced to the writer.
- Timeline metadata persists and survives refresh/reload.

## Priority rationale
Timeline support improves structural quality and reduces revision pain, making the app more credible for long-form fiction projects.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Create outline corkboard linked to scenes for planning and reordering
description:
## Problem
Writers need to plan story beats before and during drafting, but the app has no practical planning surface. Without an outline/corkboard, users bounce to external tools and lose context.

## Target user
Planner and hybrid writers who map scenes before drafting and frequently adjust sequence during revisions.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active manuscripts with at least one outline card
- Increase scene reorder actions completed inside app (vs external tools)
- Improve week-2 retention for planner/hybrid writing styles

## Scope
- Add Outline view with card-based scene summaries (one card per scene)
- Each card includes: scene title, intent, POV, and optional status tag
- Reordering cards updates the underlying scene order
- Clicking a card jumps user to that scene in editor
- Empty-outline onboarding suggests creating first 3 beats

## Non-goals
- AI-generated outlines
- Full timeline/dependency graph
- Community sharing of outlines

## Acceptance criteria
- User can create/edit outline fields per scene and data persists
- Dragging cards updates scene order reflected in chapter/scene navigator
- Card click deep-links into correct scene editor
- Status tags can be filtered (e.g., Drafted, Needs work, Final)
- Empty state includes clear call-to-action and no dead-end screen

## Priority rationale
This feature supports Living Writer-style structured planning and keeps writers inside one workflow. It strengthens differentiation from plain editors while remaining practical for MVP sequencing after core CRUD/autosave.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
